consensus_trades.catalyst: War-driven energy surge continues into April, core tariff effects emerging. Persistent inflationary pressures expected in energy and housing.
consensus_trades.risk_notes: Ceasefire could rapidly deflate energy prices; base effects from March spike. Unexpected disinflationary trends could lower CPI. Current price 0.76 exceeds max_acceptable_price 0.745 by 1.5bps—manageable given high conviction.

single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini takes opposite direction (YES on rate cuts before 2027), creating direction_mismatch. Claude's 0 cuts thesis stronger given March 3.3% CPI spike and hawkish Fed messaging. Current price 0.379 trades below max_acceptable_price—attractive entry.

Gemini targets >2.0% GDP (different threshold) with YES direction and 12% edge, contradicting Claude's NO on >1.5%. Atlanta Fed GDPNow 1.3% grounds Claude's conservative view. Gemini relies on 'strong consumer spending' without nowcast specificity. Claude's focus on war disruption in Q1 is more concrete.

Gemini did not analyze this market. Claude's 17% edge is exceptionally strong, grounded in March 3.3% YoY reading plus energy persistence. High conviction. Current price 0.57 is well below max_acceptable_price—strong entry opportunity.

contradictions.explanation: Claude explicitly trades YES on 'Fed Cut Rates 0 Times 2026' (ticker KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0) with 15% edge, forecasting zero cuts. Gemini trades YES on 'Will the Fed cut rates before 2027' with 11% edge, forecasting at least one cut. Fundamental disagreement on Fed action. Claude's thesis is grounded in recent 3.3% March CPI and hawkish Fed positioning. Gemini assumes 'expected softening of inflation pressures by late 2026' without specific inflation dynamics. Claude's inflation persistence view is more concrete given war-energy shock durability.

Claude sells >1.5% GDP (NO), Gemini buys >2.0% GDP (YES). These are different thresholds but Claude's explicit nowcast reference (Atlanta Fed GDPNow 1.3%) directly contradicts Gemini's 'strong consumer spending' assumption. Claude's war disruption catalyst is concrete; Gemini lacks specific Q1 dynamics. Ranges slightly overlap at 0.60-0.65 but directional stance differs. Claude's lower threshold makes his NO more defensible than Gemini's YES on higher threshold.

portfolio_concentration_warning: INFLATION CLUSTER EXCEEDS 5-WEIGHT LIMIT: total_weight=13 across KXCPI-26APR-T0.4 (weight 6) and KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.6 (weight 7). Both trades are highly correlated—if energy prices collapse or disinflationary surprise occurs, portfolio loses 13 points simultaneously. RECOMMENDATION: Reduce KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.6 from weight 7 to weight 3, or reduce KXCPI-26APR-T0.4 from weight 6 to weight 2. Alternatively, hedge with small NO position on inflation or long-duration nominal bonds. Current configuration violates cluster discipline. Macro regime concentration risk is severe given >60% of portfolio in inflation trades.
reeval_triggers: Ceasefire agreement in Iran conflict—energy prices collapse >5% intraday
New Fed Chair announcement with dovish signaling—triggers re-eval of rate cut markets
Atlanta Fed GDPNow revision above 2.0%—invalidates Real GDP NO thesis
Core CPI print >2.8%—suggests higher inflation persistence, strengthens KXCPIYOY case
Energy price spike >$85/barrel WTI—extends inflation duration, upgrades both CPI trades
Significant supply chain normalization signals (e.g., container shipping index down >10%)—suggests disinflationary pressure
Fed meeting minutes or Chair speech with explicit easing signals—contradicts KXRATECUTCOUNT 0-cuts thesis
